Orang-orang ini berjuang melawan orang-orang yang ditunjuk Trump -- -- bagaimana mengatakannya di Kongres? Apakah mereka takut? Apakah mereka frustrasi? Karena selalu ada orang yang berbicara di depan umum, dan ada yang berbicara dengan Anda di koridor.

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.en: Well, yes, I call them the YOLO caucus. You only live once caucus.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, saya menyebut mereka kaukus YOLO. Kaukus You Only Live Once.

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.en: The guys who actually have the freedom to talk. But listen, Cassidy may be out front there on this, but a whole lot of Republicans I speak to agree with him.
paragraphs.cn: Mereka yang benar-benar memiliki kebebasan berbicara. Tapi dengar, Cassidy mungkin yang terdepan dalam hal ini, tapi banyak Republikan yang saya ajak bicara setuju dengannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, that's the yellow caucus.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, itu kaukus kuning.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: They're the ones who are going to speak publicly.
paragraphs.cn: Mereka adalah orang-orang yang akan berbicara di depan umum.

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.en: Yes. I mean, they see a president who's more focused on, you know, renovating a golf course, as opposed to signing a housing bill, a huge bipartisan housing bill that passed Congress overwhelmingly, that still hasn't gotten signed. Why?
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Maksud saya, mereka melihat seorang presiden yang lebih fokus merenovasi lapangan golf daripada menandatangani RUU perumahan -- -- RUU perumahan bipartisan yang mendapat dukungan luar biasa di Kongres, masih belum ditandatangani. Mengapa?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Right. Well, to rob Democrats and a Senate that he's frustrated with of the photo op.
paragraphs.cn: Benar. Yah, untuk menghilangkan kesempatan Demokrat dan Senat yang tidak dia sukai untuk berfoto.

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.en: I think -- I think the fear with Republicans, it's actually much more petulant than that. And that it was a completely emotional decision by Trump because he is obsessed with the SAVE act and he denied his party wins. And, you know, Republicans are basically resigned at this point that Donald Trump is just never going to be on message throughout the midterms. He's not going to be out front talking about specific policies to bring down prices.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir -- -- Saya pikir kekhawatiran Republikan sebenarnya lebih bersifat sewenang-wenang dari itu. Ini adalah keputusan Trump yang sepenuhnya emosional karena dia terobsesi dengan SAVE Act dan menyangkal kemenangan partainya sendiri. Dan, Anda tahu, Republikan pada dasarnya sudah pasrah bahwa Donald Trump tidak akan pernah bermain sesuai aturan selama masa pemilu paruh waktu. Dia tidak akan tampil dan berbicara tentang kebijakan konkret untuk menurunkan harga.

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.en: And what you're seeing in some ways is a second term Trump that just has an exhausted legislative agenda.
paragraphs.cn: Dalam beberapa hal, Anda melihat Trump masa jabatan kedua yang agenda legislatifnya sudah mandek.

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.en: They have not really proposed any big things.
paragraphs.cn: Mereka sebenarnya tidak mengajukan inisiatif besar apa pun.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But is it because he knows he can rile up his supporters and really rally them around pet culture war issues? So, one of them, of course, is the SAVE Act, which does a myriad of other things other than voting. There's a lot of stuff stuck in there. Transgender sports. And then telling states, turn in all your voter rolls because we've got to compare notes. There's a lot of question marks there. Here's Senator Tillis on Sunday, again, part of this wounded bear caucus you're talking about.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi apakah itu karena dia tahu cara menghasut para pendukungnya untuk bersatu di sekitar isu-isu perang budaya yang dia sukai? Jadi, salah satu isu itu tentu saja SAVE Act, yang mencakup banyak hal selain pemungutan suara. Banyak barang yang dimasukkan ke dalamnya. Olahraga transgender. Lalu meminta negara bagian menyerahkan semua daftar pemilih, karena kami akan mencocokkan informasi. Ada banyak tanda tanya di sini. Ini adalah bagian dari pidato Senator Tillis hari Minggu, yang termasuk dalam kategori "beruang terluka" yang Anda sebutkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But here's how he sees this focus. SEN. THOM TILLIS (R-NC): Why are we doing more things to undermine our confidence in elections, rather than getting the strong message out that we'll win for Republicans this year? Talk about the emergence and the rise of the Democratic Socialists of America. Accept that the voting laws are going to be fundamentally what they are today. Win by them. Win buy the good results that Republicans have produced and stop undermining the confidence in the elections.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi beginilah cara dia melihat fokus ini. Senator Thom Tillis (R-NC): Mengapa kita melakukan lebih banyak hal yang merusak kepercayaan pada pemilu, bukannya mengirimkan pesan kuat bahwa kita akan menang untuk Partai Republik tahun ini? Bicarakan tentang kebangkitan Sosialis Demokrat Amerika. Menerima undang-undang pemilu pada dasarnya akan mempertahankan status quo. Menang dengan undang-undang itu. Menang dengan hasil baik yang dicapai Partai Republik, berhenti merusak kepercayaan pada pemilu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The argument is not crazy, considering that just over the weekend a bunch of Democrats released a promise to America, we are capitalist, not socialist. Manifesto, if you will.
paragraphs.cn: Argumen itu tidak gila, mengingat akhir pekan lalu, sekelompok Demokrat merilis "Janji kepada Amerika": Kami adalah kapitalis, bukan sosialis. Bisa dibilang sebuah manifesto.

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.en: A capitalist manifesto.
paragraphs.cn: Sebuah manifesto kapitalis.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, a capitalist manifesto, ironically. But does it show that even though Trump's not on message, Democrats are not exactly getting to November together?
paragraphs.cn: Ya, ironisnya, sebuah manifesto kapitalis. Tapi apakah ini menunjukkan bahwa meskipun Trump tidak bermain sesuai aturan, Demokrat juga tidak sepenuhnya bersatu menuju November?

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.en: Yes, everything's not going well for the Democrats right now for a number of reasons. I mean, the problem we in -- they have in Maine as well as a -- is an issue. I think --
paragraphs.cn: Ya, karena beberapa alasan, Demokrat tidak dalam posisi yang baik saat ini. Maksud saya, kami di Maine dan masalah yang mereka hadapi adalah sebuah masalah. Saya pikir -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The Maine Senate race.
paragraphs.cn: Pemilihan Senator Maine.

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.en: The Maine Senate race. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: Pemilihan Senator Maine. Ya.

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.en: The thing with Trump is, he's not convinced. And he is -- his aides have tried to convince him -- that the midterm elections are really something he should devote his energy to.
paragraphs.cn: Masalah Trump adalah dia tidak yakin. Para pembantunya mencoba meyakinkannya bahwa pemilu paruh waktu memang sesuatu yang harus dia fokuskan.

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.en: He's not sure. He basically assumes he's going to lose the House. He doesn't think it matters much. So, he's trying to figure out what he can do -- what leverage he has here. And right now he's taking his own party hostage. I mean that housing bill is a messaging bill that's supposed to help Republicans go home and say, look, I do care about affordability. I'm doing stuff for you. He's hurting his own party because he thinks he can get the SAVE Act passed. The SAVE Act pass -- is not going to pass.
paragraphs.cn: Dia tidak yakin. Dia pada dasarnya berasumsi akan kehilangan DPR. Dia pikir itu tidak penting. Jadi, dia mencoba mencari tahu apa yang bisa dia lakukan -- -- apa pengaruhnya di sini. Dan sekarang, dia menyandera partainya sendiri. Maksud saya, RUU perumahan itu adalah RUU pesan yang dimaksudkan untuk membantu Republikan pulang dan berkata, lihat, saya peduli dengan keterjangkauan. Saya bekerja untuk Anda. Dia menyakiti partainya sendiri karena dia pikir dia bisa meloloskan SAVE Act. SAVE Act tidak akan lolos.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. Which Senator Thune and many other Republicans have said, we're not voting for it. Democrats surely are not.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Senator Thune dan banyak Republikan lainnya telah mengatakan, kami tidak akan memilihnya. Demokrat tentu saja juga tidak.

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.en: Well, I was just going to add, you know, he may think that he can stir up his base, but his own political team in that Maggie Haberman, Jonathan Swan book, his own political team has written memos back in December that he -- that voters feel that he is not focused enough on cost of living. And that was even before the Iran War.
paragraphs.cn: Yah, saya hanya ingin menambahkan, Anda tahu, dia mungkin berpikir dia bisa menghasut basisnya, tapi menurut buku Maggie Haberman dan Jonathan Swan, tim politiknya sendiri menulis memo pada bulan Desember lalu yang mengatakan pemilih menganggap dia tidak cukup fokus pada biaya hidup. Itu bahkan sebelum perang Iran.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's before they get on the road and pay for gas and buy snacks and find all the expensing. All right, so, Alex, since you're back, it's been a while, what's in your group chat?
paragraphs.cn: Itu sebelum mereka naik kendaraan, membayar bensin, membeli camilan, dan menemukan semua pengeluaran itu. Oke, Alex, karena Anda kembali, sudah lama tidak bertemu, apa yang ada di grup chat Anda?

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.en: The new Larry David show, which basically drops "Curb Your Enthusiasm" into huge American history moment.
paragraphs.cn: Acara Larry David yang baru pada dasarnya memasukkan Curb Your Enthusiasm ke dalam momen-momen besar sejarah Amerika.

paragraphs.speaker: Thompson
paragraphs.en: For example, you have a -- you have Rosa Parks refusing to go to the back of the bus, but then Larry David sits next to her and annoys her so much that she ends up going back to the -- to the back of the bus. Stuff like that.
paragraphs.cn: Misalnya, Rosa Parks menolak duduk di belakang bus, tetapi Larry David duduk di sebelahnya dan membuatnya sangat kesal sehingga akhirnya dia kembali ke belakang bus. Hal-hal seperti itu.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, of course. Yes, stuff like that. Michael, what about you? What's in your group chat?
paragraphs.cn: Ya, tentu. Ya, hal serupa. Michael, bagaimana denganmu? Apa yang ada di grup chatmu?

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.en: I watched it over the weekend. It was very. I -- mine's a little less funny. I think the degree to which the Democrats are going back to their old pattern of moving to the left in a way that is going to endanger them is just a huge story that is just now emerging.
paragraphs.cn: Saya menontonnya akhir pekan lalu. Sangat -- grup chat saya tidak semenarik itu. Saya pikir Partai Demokrat kembali ke pola lama mereka, yaitu berbelok ke kiri, dan ini akan membahayakan mereka sendiri, ini adalah berita besar yang baru muncul.

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.en: We had two years of Democrats saying we were wrong on trans, we were wrong on immigration issues, and now we're back to discussions about whether the police are good or not, about how we should pack the Supreme Court, about --
paragraphs.cn: Selama dua tahun, Partai Demokrat mengatakan kami salah tentang isu transgender, salah tentang imigrasi, dan sekarang kami kembali ke diskusi tentang apakah polisi itu baik, tentang apakah kami harus memperluas Mahkamah Agung, tentang --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, which will be familiar if you're like in the Romney campaign or something. I just want to show this "Axios" headline, "Dems Tea Party-Like Rebellion Built by a Decade of Frustration." This is the conversation. When you say moving to the left, some of them think, well, maybe we're just doing what MAGA did.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, jika Anda mengikuti kampanye Romney dll, Anda akan merasa seperti pernah melihatnya. Saya hanya ingin menunjukkan judul Axios: 'Pemberontakan ala Tea Party Demokrat terakumulasi dari frustrasi selama sepuluh tahun.' Itulah percakapannya. Ketika Anda mengatakan berbelok ke kiri, beberapa orang berpikir, mungkin kita hanya melakukan apa yang dilakukan MAGA.

paragraphs.speaker: Scherer
paragraphs.en: No. And if you remember back in 2018, 2019, it really hurt Democrats going into 2020. And it -- and it helped set the stage for a lot of the mistakes that Joe Biden made in his first couple years of office.
paragraphs.cn: Tidak. Jika Anda ingat tahun 2018, 2019, itu benar-benar merugikan Partai Demokrat sebelum pemilu 2020. Dan -- dan itu menjadi dasar bagi banyak kesalahan yang dibuat Joe Biden di tahun-tahun awal pemerintahannya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right. What's in your group chat, Igor?
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah. Igor, apa yang ada di grup chatmu?

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.en: My chat is dominated by the World Cup right now.
paragraphs.cn: Grup chat saya sekarang terutama tentang Piala Dunia.

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.en: I am going to the USA versus Bosnia game, which is --
paragraphs.cn: Saya akan menonton pertandingan AS vs Bosnia, itu --

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.en: And having been born in Bosnia, this is going to be a really tough decision for me. Basically, whoever wins, I lose or I win. I don't know. I haven't decided.
paragraphs.cn: Dan karena saya lahir di Bosnia, ini akan menjadi keputusan yang sangat sulit bagi saya. Pada dasarnya, siapa pun yang menang, saya kalah atau menang. Saya tidak tahu. Saya belum memutuskan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Wow. Oh my gosh. Wow.
paragraphs.cn: Wow. Ya ampun. Wow.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I'm excited for you.
paragraphs.cn: Saya senang untukmu.

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.en: Yes. Thank you so much. There's a lot to cheer for. One of the -- our players on the Bosnian national team, Esmir Bajraktarevic, was born in Wisconsin. He's the son of Bosnian refugees who --
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Terima kasih banyak. Ada banyak hal untuk dirayakan. Salah satu pemain tim nasional Bosnia kami, Esmir Bajraktarević, lahir di Wisconsin. Dia adalah putra pengungsi Bosnia, mereka --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I like that you said that name, but in a Wisconsin accent.
paragraphs.cn: Saya suka cara Anda mengucapkan nama itu, tetapi dengan aksen Wisconsin.

paragraphs.speaker: Bobic
paragraphs.en: That's right. Listen, he -- his parents, you know, came from the Srebrenica genocide. So, there's a lot of tragedy there. This country is pumped up. First time that it's made the knockout stages. We're all rooting for them. It's going to be tough for me, though. (INAUDIBLE).
paragraphs.cn: Benar. Dengar, dia -- orang tuanya, Anda tahu, melarikan diri dari genosida Srebrenica. Jadi, ada banyak tragedi di sana. Negara ini sekarang sangat bersemangat. Ini pertama kalinya mereka lolos ke babak gugur. Kami semua mendukung mereka. Tapi ini sulit bagi saya. (Tidak jelas).

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Oh, love this. Igor, so glad to have you here. Thank you so much. Thank you guys for being in the group chat today. And thank you for kicking off the week with us. We've got the headlines coming up next. I'm Audie Cornish. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Oh, saya suka ini. Igor, senang Anda ada di sini. Terima kasih banyak. Terima kasih telah bergabung dalam grup chat hari ini. Dan terima kasih telah memulai minggu ini bersama kami. Selanjutnya, berita utama. Saya Audi Cornish.

vocabulary.en: to withdraw or retreat from a position or conflict
vocabulary.excerpt: Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow after exchanging fire near the Strait of Hormuz. Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow after exchanging fire near the Strait of Hormuz.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Amerika Serikat dan Iran, setelah baku tembak di dekat Selat Hormuz, setuju untuk bertemu besok di Qatar.

vocabulary.en: a temporary suspension of fighting, typically one during which peace talks take place
vocabulary.excerpt: Is this ceasefire collapsing?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Apakah perjanjian gencatan senjata ini sedang runtuh?

vocabulary.en: waste or rough fragments of stone, brick, concrete, etc., especially after demolition or an earthquake
vocabulary.excerpt: Babies buried under piles of debris found alive, days after dual mass earthquakes in Venezuela.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Beberapa hari setelah gempa bumi ganda di Venezuela, bayi yang terkubur di bawah tumpukan puing ditemukan masih hidup.

vocabulary.en: extremely hot weather
vocabulary.excerpt: And fires turn deadly in Utah. Now, scorching heat is about to make things a little harder for firefighters.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Kebakaran di Utah menjadi mematikan. Sekarang, panas terik akan membuat pekerjaan petugas pemadam kebakaran semakin sulit.

vocabulary.cn: pihak yang lemah, pihak yang tidak diunggulkan
vocabulary.en: a competitor thought to have little chance of winning a fight or contest
vocabulary.excerpt: And everyone loves an underdog. Does the U.S. have a shot at winning the World Cup?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Semua orang menyukai pihak yang lemah. Apakah tim AS memiliki peluang untuk memenangkan Piala Dunia?

vocabulary.cn: operasi pembersihan, operasi militer untuk membersihkan sisa musuh
vocabulary.en: a military operation to clear an area of remaining enemy forces after a major battle
vocabulary.excerpt: The major war is over. And think of this as almost just a mop-up operation.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Perang utama sudah berakhir. Anggap ini hampir sebagai operasi pembersihan.

vocabulary.en: an agreement between enemies or opponents to stop fighting for a certain time
vocabulary.excerpt: Could the truce be on the verge of crumbling?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Apakah perjanjian gencatan senjata hampir runtuh?

vocabulary.en: very close to; about to
vocabulary.excerpt: Could the truce be on the verge of crumbling?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Apakah perjanjian gencatan senjata hampir runtuh?

vocabulary.level: Latar belakang istilah khusus · Tingkat lanjut
vocabulary.cn: Selat Hormuz (jalur transportasi minyak penting yang menghubungkan Teluk Persia dan Laut Arab)
vocabulary.en: a narrow strait between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman, a vital oil shipping route
vocabulary.excerpt: Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow after exchanging fire near the Strait of Hormuz.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Setelah baku tembak antara Amerika Serikat dan Iran di dekat Selat Hormuz, mereka setuju untuk bertemu besok di Qatar.

vocabulary.cn: Target yang ditentukan, target yang diharapkan
vocabulary.en: the targets that were planned to be hit
vocabulary.excerpt: but a U.S. official says no Iranian drones or missiles hit their intended targets
vocabulary.exampleCn: Namun seorang pejabat AS mengatakan bahwa drone atau rudal Iran tidak mengenai target yang ditentukan.

vocabulary.en: breaking or failing to comply with (a rule or formal agreement)
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump threatened that, if Iran continued violating the ceasefire, it, quote, 'will no longer exist.'
vocabulary.exampleCn: Presiden Trump mengancam bahwa jika Iran terus melanggar perjanjian gencatan senjata, Iran akan 'tidak ada lagi'.

vocabulary.en: the transport of goods by sea between countries
vocabulary.excerpt: while Iran continues to attack international shipping without a response
vocabulary.exampleCn: Sementara Iran terus menyerang pelayaran internasional tanpa mendapat respons.

vocabulary.en: the basic physical and organizational structures needed for the operation of a society or enterprise
vocabulary.excerpt: take down their infrastructure that they're trying to use to illegally control an international waterway
vocabulary.exampleCn: Menghancurkan infrastruktur yang mereka coba gunakan untuk menguasai jalur air internasional secara ilegal.

vocabulary.en: short, unplanned fights, especially between small groups of soldiers
vocabulary.excerpt: They are OK with the level of violence and these skirmishes, these exchange of attacks
vocabulary.exampleCn: Mereka menerima tingkat kekerasan ini dan pertukaran bentrokan kecil serta serangan ini.

vocabulary.cn: Garis merah, batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar
vocabulary.en: a figurative line that should not be crossed, especially in diplomacy or conflict
vocabulary.excerpt: as they try and set their red lines and try and shape negotiations
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ketika mereka mencoba menetapkan garis merah dan membentuk negosiasi.

vocabulary.en: the period of time that can be predicted with reasonable certainty
vocabulary.excerpt: I think we're going to see these for the foreseeable future.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Saya pikir kita akan melihat ini di masa depan yang dapat diperkirakan.

vocabulary.en: the most important and practical details of a subject or situation
vocabulary.excerpt: And I'm under the impression this is the part that really gets into the nitty gritty of what happens to enriched uranium.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Kesan saya adalah bahwa bagian ini benar-benar menyentuh detail substansial tentang ke mana perginya uranium yang diperkaya.

vocabulary.cn: Uranium yang diperkaya (uranium dengan kandungan U-235 yang ditingkatkan, digunakan untuk reaktor nuklir atau senjata nuklir)
vocabulary.en: uranium in which the proportion of the isotope uranium-235 has been increased, used as fuel in nuclear reactors or in nuclear weapons
vocabulary.excerpt: this is the part that really gets into the nitty gritty of what happens to enriched uranium.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Bagian ini benar-benar menyentuh detail substansial tentang ke mana perginya uranium yang diperkaya.

vocabulary.en: to use power or influence to control something
vocabulary.excerpt: you see Iran trying to exert its control over the Strait of Hormuz
vocabulary.exampleCn: Anda melihat Iran mencoba menerapkan kendali atas Selat Hormuz.

vocabulary.en: the power to influence a person or situation to achieve a desired outcome
vocabulary.excerpt: Iran is trying to retain leverage over the Strait of Hormuz, its major lever that it has had during this war
vocabulary.exampleCn: Iran mencoba mempertahankan pengaruh atas Selat Hormuz, yang merupakan pengaruh utama yang dimilikinya selama perang.

vocabulary.en: things that are granted or yielded, especially in negotiations
vocabulary.excerpt: whether or not Iran, through this process, is actually getting concessions.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 伊朗是否通过这一过程实际上获得了让步。

vocabulary.en: an unexpected, unearned, or sudden gain or advantage
vocabulary.excerpt: because the Iranians want to keep getting the windfall that they've gotten out of this MOU.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 因为伊朗人想继续从这份谅解备忘录中获得意外之财。

vocabulary.en: a document describing a bilateral or multilateral agreement between parties, not legally binding
vocabulary.excerpt: the windfall that they've gotten out of this MOU.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们从这份谅解备忘录中获得的意外之财。

vocabulary.cn: 补充战争资金（额外拨款的军事预算）
vocabulary.en: additional money allocated by a government to cover the costs of a war beyond the regular budget
vocabulary.excerpt: the U.S. wants to be at the table, but they're asking for supplemental war funding of Congress to do it to the tune of 87.6 billion.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 美国想留在谈判桌上，但他们要求国会提供876亿美元的补充战争资金。

sentences.en: Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow after exchanging fire near the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 美国和伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡附近交火后，同意明天在卡塔尔会面。
sentences.structure: 主句：Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow；时间状语：after exchanging fire near the Strait of Hormuz。
sentences.analysis: 现在分词短语作主句谓语，表示主动和进行；after + 动名词短语作时间状语，表示先后顺序。

sentences.en: Iran targeted U.S. allies in Bahrain and Kuwait, but a U.S. official says no Iranian drones or missiles hit their intended targets, saying, quote, "To be clear, Iran failed."
sentences.cn: 伊朗袭击了美国在巴林和科威特的盟友，但一名美国官员表示，没有伊朗无人机或导弹击中预定目标，并引用说："明确地说，伊朗失败了。"
sentences.structure: 并列句：Iran targeted...but a U.S. official says...；saying...为现在分词短语作伴随状语，quote引导直接引语。
sentences.analysis: but连接转折关系；saying...补充说明官员的言论；quote用于新闻中引入直接引语。

sentences.en: President Trump threatened that, if Iran continued violating the ceasefire, it, quote, "will no longer exist."
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统威胁说，如果伊朗继续违反停火协议，它"将不复存在"。
sentences.structure: 主句：President Trump threatened that...；宾语从句：if Iran continued violating the ceasefire, it will no longer exist；插入语：quote。
sentences.analysis: if引导条件状语从句；quote表示直接引用；"will no longer exist"是威胁性表述。

sentences.en: If the Iranian regime thinks for a second that President Trump is going to sit -- sit by, stand by while Iran continues to attack international shipping without a response or our bases without a response, they're sadly mistaken.
sentences.cn: 如果伊朗政权有一秒钟认为特朗普总统会袖手旁观，坐视伊朗继续攻击国际航运或我们的基地而不作出回应，那他们就大错特错了。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句：If the Iranian regime thinks...；宾语从句：that President Trump is going to sit by, stand by...；主句：they're sadly mistaken。
sentences.analysis: sit by和stand by同义重复，强调不作为；while引导时间状语从句；without a response作状语修饰attack。

sentences.en: And they saw that loud and clear over the last few nights, where we'll continue to militarily, if needed, take down their infrastructure that they're trying to use to illegally control an international waterway.
sentences.cn: 他们在过去几个晚上清楚地看到了这一点，我们将继续在必要时采取军事行动，摧毁他们试图用来非法控制国际水道的设施。
sentences.structure: 主句：And they saw that loud and clear over the last few nights；where引导非限制性定语从句修饰nights；从句中if needed为插入条件状语；that引导定语从句修饰infrastructure。
sentences.analysis: loud and clear为固定短语，意为"清楚地"；militarily为副词修饰take down；if needed为省略句。

sentences.en: I think both Tehran and Washington have decided that the ceasefire is more fire than cease.
sentences.cn: 我认为德黑兰和华盛顿都认定，这场停火与其说是停火，不如说是交火。
sentences.structure: 主句：I think；宾语从句：both Tehran and Washington have decided that...；that引导的宾语从句中：the ceasefire is more fire than cease。
sentences.analysis: more...than...结构表示"与其说……不如说……"，fire和cease形成对比，fire指交火，cease指停止。

sentences.en: They are OK with the level of violence and these skirmishes, these exchange of attacks, as they try and set their red lines and try and shape negotiations, frankly, via these attacks.
sentences.cn: 他们接受这种程度的暴力和这些小规模冲突、这些攻击交换，因为他们试图通过这些攻击来设定红线并塑造谈判，坦率地说。
sentences.structure: 主句：They are OK with...；as引导原因状语从句；从句中try and set...and try and shape...为并列谓语；frankly为插入语。
sentences.analysis: be OK with表示"接受"；skirmishes和exchange of attacks为同位语；via these attacks作方式状语。

sentences.en: And right now, there is a commitment to continue talking at the same time that they're going to continue shooting.
sentences.cn: 而现在，他们承诺继续谈判，同时他们也将继续开火。
sentences.structure: 主句：there is a commitment to continue talking；at the same time that引导时间状语从句。
sentences.analysis: commitment to do sth.表示"做某事的承诺"；at the same time that表示"在……的同时"。

sentences.en: And I'm under the impression this is the part that really gets into the nitty gritty of what happens to enriched uranium.
sentences.cn: 我的印象是，这部分真正涉及浓缩铀去向的具体细节。
sentences.structure: 主句：I'm under the impression；同位语从句：this is the part...；that引导定语从句修饰part；of what happens...为介词短语作定语修饰nitty gritty。
sentences.analysis: under the impression that...意为"有……的印象"；nitty gritty为俚语，意为"具体细节"。

sentences.en: So, Iran is trying to retain leverage over the Strait of Hormuz, its major lever that it has had during this war, without --
sentences.cn: 因此，伊朗正试图保持对霍尔木兹海峡的影响力，这是它在战争期间拥有的主要杠杆，而无需 -- --
sentences.structure: 主句：Iran is trying to retain leverage over the Strait of Hormuz；its major lever为同位语；that引导定语从句修饰lever；without后省略。
sentences.analysis: retain leverage over意为"保持对……的影响力"；lever比喻"杠杆，手段"。

sentences.en: The Iranians are leaning forward to exercise control over the strait to remind the world that they control that waterway, then, leaning back when the Trump administration objects vigorously enough, because the Iranians want to keep getting the windfall that they've gotten out of this MOU.
sentences.cn: 伊朗人向前倾，以行使对海峡的控制权，提醒世界他们控制着那条水道，然后，当特朗普政府足够强烈地反对时，他们又向后靠，因为伊朗人想继续从这份谅解备忘录中获得意外之财。
sentences.structure: 并列结构：leaning forward...then leaning back...；leaning forward后接不定式表目的；when引导时间状语从句；because引导原因状语从句；that引导定语从句修饰windfall。
sentences.analysis: lean forward/back比喻"向前/后退"；exercise control over意为"行使对……的控制"；windfall意为"意外之财"；MOU为Memorandum of Understanding缩写。

sentences.en: And then when it comes to the nuclear file, I think they will dribble out very small concessions bit by bit, then pull them back, then put them forward, then pull them back to keep the United States at the table.
sentences.cn: 然后当谈到核问题时，我认为他们会一点一点地挤出非常小的让步，然后收回，再提出，再收回，以让美国留在谈判桌上。
sentences.structure: 时间状语从句：when it comes to the nuclear file；主句：I think；宾语从句：they will dribble out...；并列谓语：dribble out, pull back, put forward, pull back；不定式表目的：to keep...。
sentences.analysis: when it comes to...意为"当谈到……"；dribble out意为"一点点地放出"；bit by bit意为"一点一点地"；keep...at the table意为"让……留在谈判桌上"。

sentences.en: Democrats are obviously very opposed to this to begin with, but I think the fraying of this ceasefire -- or not ceasefire or whatever you want to call it right now -- is just going to intensify questions in Congress about this huge amount of money they want.
sentences.cn: 民主党人显然从一开始就非常反对这一点，但我认为，这场停火 -- -- 或者不是停火，或者你现在想怎么称呼它 -- -- 的破裂只会加剧国会对他们想要这笔巨额资金的质疑。
sentences.structure: 并列句：Democrats are opposed...but I think...；think后宾语从句：the fraying...is going to intensify questions...；破折号内为插入语；they want为定语从句修饰money。
sentences.analysis: to begin with意为"首先，一开始"；fraying意为"磨损，破裂"；or not ceasefire or whatever为口语化插入语。

sentences.en: Democrats see blood in the water, and they're going to make them pay in November for this war that's unpopular across both parties.
sentences.cn: 民主党人看到了机会，他们要让共和党人在11月为这场在两党都不受欢迎的战争付出代价。
sentences.structure: 并列句：Democrats see blood...and they're going to make them pay...；that引导定语从句修饰war。
sentences.analysis: see blood in the water为习语，意为"看到机会（尤指对手的弱点）"；make sb. pay意为"让某人付出代价"；across both parties意为"在两党中"。

sentences.en: In our reporting -- and we write about this in the book -- none of Trump's senior advisors, none of his cabinet, really thought this was a good idea.
sentences.cn: 在我们的报道中 -- -- 我们在书中也写到了这一点 -- -- 特朗普的高级顾问、他的内阁成员，没有人真的认为这是个好主意。
sentences.structure: 主句：none of Trump's senior advisors...thought this was a good idea；破折号内为插入语。
sentences.analysis: none of...none of...双重否定强调无人；插入语补充说明。

sentences.en: But Vance was the only one who really vocally took issue with this, with Trump.
sentences.cn: 但万斯是唯一一个真正公开对此提出异议的人，与特朗普意见相左。
sentences.structure: 主句：But Vance was the only one；who引导定语从句修饰one；with Trump为状语。
sentences.analysis: vocally意为"直言地"；take issue with意为"对……提出异议"。

sentences.en: And it irritated Trump.
sentences.cn: 这激怒了特朗普。
sentences.structure: 简单句。
sentences.analysis: irritate意为"激怒"。

sentences.en: I think you're seeing a larger I mean, what she is talking about is a larger dynamic in this White House, is that people do not want to give Trump bad news.
sentences.cn: 我认为你看到的是一个更大的 -- -- 我的意思是，她所谈论的是白宫中一个更大的动态，那就是人们不想给特朗普带来坏消息。
sentences.structure: 主句：I think；宾语从句：you're seeing a larger...；插入语：I mean；what引导主语从句；that引导表语从句。
sentences.analysis: I mean为口语插入语；dynamic意为"动态，互动模式"。

sentences.en: And you're seeing Trump having -- trotting -- trotting out Vance and being like, Well, now you fix it.
sentences.cn: 而且你看到特朗普让万斯出来 -- -- 出来 -- -- 然后说，好吧，现在你来解决它。
sentences.structure: 主句：you're seeing Trump having...；现在分词短语trotting out Vance作宾语补足语；being like引导直接引语。
sentences.analysis: trot out意为"推出，展示（常含贬义）"；being like为口语用法，意为"说"。

sentences.en: You didn't even want to go in, but now it's your responsibility to fix this thing.
sentences.cn: 你当初甚至不想介入，但现在解决这件事是你的责任。
sentences.structure: 并列句：You didn't even want to go in, but now it's your responsibility to fix this thing。
sentences.analysis: go in意为"介入"；it's one's responsibility to do sth.意为"做某事是某人的责任"。

topics.title: 美伊冲突与霍尔木兹海峡
topics.content: 霍尔木兹海峡（Strait of Hormuz）是连接波斯湾和阿拉伯海的狭窄水道，全球约20%的石油运输经过此处。伊朗常以控制该海峡作为地缘政治杠杆。2026年美伊冲突中，双方在附近交火后同意暂时停火，但伊朗试图通过限制航运路线维持影响力。
topics.keywords: Strait of Hormuz · ceasefire · leverage · international shipping

topics.title: 美国国会战争资金争议
topics.content: 美国行政分支向国会请求补充战争资金（supplemental war funding）用于军事行动。2026年美伊冲突中，特朗普政府要求876亿美元，但国会两党对此分歧严重。民主党借战争导致油价上涨、民众不满，在中期选举前施压共和党。
topics.keywords: supplemental war funding · Congress · gas prices · midterm elections

topics.title: 委内瑞拉双重地震与救援
topics.content: 2026年6月，委内瑞拉发生两次大规模地震（dual mass earthquakes），造成大量建筑倒塌。救援人员在废墟（rubble）中发现多名幸存婴儿，被称为'奇迹'。此类灾害凸显发展中国家应急响应能力的挑战。
topics.keywords: dual mass earthquakes · rubble · rescue · Venezuela

topics.title: 美国队世界杯前景
topics.content: 2026年世界杯淘汰赛阶段，美国队（Team USA）对阵波黑队（Bosnia）。作为underdog（不被看好的一方），美国队面临强敌，但此前表现令部分球迷惊喜。世界杯是全球最受关注的体育赛事之一，美国队晋级前景引发讨论。
topics.keywords: World Cup · underdog · Team USA · Bosnia

quiz.question: 原文中 'stand down' 的含义是什么？
quiz.explanation: 在军事语境中，'stand down' 意为退出或停止行动。原文 'Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to stand down for now' 指双方同意暂时停止冲突。

quiz.question: 短语 'on the verge of' 在 'Could the truce be on the verge of crumbling?' 中的意思是？
quiz.explanation: 'on the verge of' berarti 'di ambang, akan segera', menunjukkan sesuatu akan segera terjadi. Di sini merujuk pada perjanjian gencatan senjata yang hampir runtuh.

quiz.question: Apa kepanjangan dan arti dari 'MOU' dalam teks asli?
options: A. Memorandum of Understanding, nota kesepahaman
B. Ministry of Unity, Kementerian Persatuan
C. Major Operation Unit, Unit Operasi Utama
D. Monetary Overhaul Unit, Unit Reformasi Moneter
quiz.explanation: MOU adalah singkatan dari Memorandum of Understanding, yaitu nota kesepahaman yang tidak mengikat secara hukum. Teks menyebut Iran mendapat windfall (keuntungan tak terduga) dari MOU.

quiz.question: Menurut teks, apa yang disepakati AS dan Iran setelah baku tembak?
quiz.explanation: Teks dengan jelas menyatakan 'Both the U.S. and Iran agreeing to meet in Qatar tomorrow after exchanging fire', yaitu setuju bertemu di Qatar.

quiz.question: Bagaimana sikap anggota Partai Demokrat di Kongres terhadap pendanaan perang tambahan?
options: B. Menentang dan memanfaatkannya untuk menekan
D. Meminta jumlah yang lebih besar
quiz.explanation: Teks menyebut 'Democrats see blood in the water, and they're going to make them pay in November', menunjukkan Demokrat menentang dan memanfaatkan ketidakpopuleran perang untuk menekan.

quiz.question: Menurut buku baru Maggie Haberman, bagaimana sikap para penasihat Trump terhadap perang?
options: B. 多数认为不是好主意，但只有万斯公开反对
quiz.explanation: 原文引用 Haberman 说 'none of Trump's senior advisors... really thought this was a good idea. But Vance was the only one who really vocally took issue'，即多数不赞成，但只有万斯公开反对。
